source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
title: Ensure use of Google-managed SSL Certificates

input_row.source: CIS GOOGLE KUBERNETES ENGINE (GKE) BENCHMARK V1.7.0 PDF
input_row.title: Ensure use of Google-managed SSL Certificates
input_row.description: Encrypt traffic to HTTPS load balancers using Google-managed SSL certificates.
input_row.rationale: Encrypting traffic between users and the Kubernetes workload is fundamental to protecting data sent over the web. Google-managed SSL Certificates are provisioned, renewed, and managed for domain names. This is only available for HTTPS load balancers created using Ingress Resources, and not TCP/UDP load balancers created using Service of type:LoadBalancer. Impact: Google-managed SSL Certificates are less flexible than certificates that are self obtained and managed. Managed certificates support a single, non-wildcard domain. Self-managed certificates can support wildcards and multiple subject alternative names (SANs).
input_row.audit: Using Command Line: Identify if there are any workloads exposed publicly using Services of type:LoadBalancer: kubectl get svc -A -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.type=="LoadBalancer")' Consider using ingresses instead of these services in order to use Google managed SSL certificates. For the ingresses within the cluster, run the following command: kubectl get ingress -A -o json | jq .items[] | jq '{name: .metadata.name, annotations: .metadata.annotations, namespace: .metadata.namespace, status: .status}' The above command should return the name of the ingress, namespace, annotations and status. Check that the following annotation is present to ensure managed certificates are referenced. "annotations": { ... "networking.gke.io/managed-certificates": "<example_certificate>" }, For completeness, run the following command to ensure that the managed certificate resource exists: kubectl get managedcertificates -A The above command returns a list of managed certificates for which <example_certificate> should exist within the same namespace as the ingress.
input_row.remediation: If services of type:LoadBalancer are discovered, consider replacing the Service with an Ingress. To configure the Ingress and use Google-managed SSL certificates, follow the instructions as listed at: https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how- to/managed-certs. Default Value: By default, Google-managed SSL Certificates are not created when an Ingress resource is defined. References: 1. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/how-to/managed-certs 2. https://cloud.google.com/kubernetes-engine/docs/concepts/ingress
input_row.impact: Google-managed SSL Certificates are less flexible than certificates that are self obtained and managed. Managed certificates support a single, non-wildcard domain. Self-managed certificates can support wildcards and multiple subject alternative names (SANs).

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:**
The control "Ensure use of Google-managed SSL Certificates" can be fully verified through available GCP APIs and commands from the Kubernetes command-line interface (kubectl). The audit method specifies using the `kubectl` commands to check for services of type `LoadBalancer` and to verify that proper annotations are present on Ingress resources for Google-managed SSL Certificates, as well as listing ManagedCertificate resources. These checks do not require manual interpretation or SSH access, thus they align well with an automated approach.

**PROGRAM NAME:** gcp_gke_ingress_google_managed_ssl_certificates_enforced

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Use the Kubernetes command-line tool (`kubectl`) to retrieve a list of all services and filter those of type `LoadBalancer` within GKE clusters.
  - Command: `kubectl get svc -A -o json | jq '.items[] | select(.spec.type=="LoadBalancer")'`
- For each Ingress resource in the cluster, verify annotations for Google-managed certificates.
  - Command: `kubectl get ingress -A -o json | jq .items[] | jq '{name: .metadata.name, annotations: .metadata.annotations, namespace: .metadata.namespace}'`
  - Check for the annotation `"networking.gke.io/managed-certificates": "<example_certificate>"` to confirm that a Google-managed SSL Certificate is in use.
- Confirm that the ManagedCertificate resource exists as configured.
  - Command: `kubectl get managedcertificates -A`
- Pass Criteria:
  - No services of type `LoadBalancer` without corresponding annotations and ManagedCertificate resources.
  - All Ingress resources must have properly configured annotations for managed certificates.
  
These automated steps ensure that the GCP GKE Ingress resources are utilizing Google-managed SSL certificates as specified by the CIS benchmark and can be effectively automated without manual intervention.
